excerpt: HRW describes El Geneina as home to an ethnically mixed population of around 540,000 and says the April-June violence lasted seven weeks, peaking in mid-June.
transform_note: Used as the pre-violence city baseline before displacement adjustment.
confidence_note: Strong baseline for city size, but not a time-weighted person-month denominator.

excerpt: Sudanese Red Crescent staff said that on June 13 they counted 2,000 bodies on El Geneina's streets and then stopped counting; HRW notes the larger June 15 massacre came afterward.
transform_note: Treated as a hard lower-bound civilian-fatality datapoint, not a full episode total.
confidence_note: Strong minimum, but clearly incomplete.

geographic_scope: El Geneina city and immediate flight corridor
excerpt: A field-hospital operator shared 880+ named dead through June 9 and estimated the full toll at more than 4,000 dead with at least 10,000 injured.
transform_note: Best value centers Reuters' 'more than 4,000' wording rather than treating 4,001 as the full answer.
confidence_note: Useful central anchor, but still monitor/witness based rather than a complete registry.

excerpt: Reuters reports that more than 290,000 people fled El Geneina to Chad and that the city had about half a million people before the violence.
transform_note: Used as displacement context to lower the April-June civilian person-month denominator; not all departures necessarily occurred before June 22.
confidence_note: Strong evidence of mass outflow, but timing is coarser than the episode window.

excerpt: Reuters says at least 1,000 bodies were buried in Al Ghabat cemetery alone over more than seven weeks, with evidence of additional mass graves elsewhere in the city.
transform_note: Used as one cemetery-specific minimum, not a citywide total.
confidence_note: High confidence as a minimum burial count for one site only.

geographic_scope: El Shate/Al Imtidad/El Nesseem access routes toward Ardamata, El Geneina
excerpt: OHCHR reports recovery and burial of 24 bodies from El Shate/Al Imtidad and 46 from El Nesseem/Al Imtidad of people killed while trying to reach Ardamata on June 15.
transform_note: Summed partial incident count for a single day's attempted escape inside the city.
confidence_note: Incident-specific and clearly incomplete for the wider episode.

geographic_scope: Ranga/Red Soil mass grave site outside El Geneina
excerpt: OHCHR says at least 87 ethnic Masalit and others killed by RSF and allied militia around June 13-21 were buried in a mass grave outside El Geneina.
transform_note: Incident count used as a lower-bound datapoint and subgroup source, not as the full episode total.
confidence_note: High confidence for the incident itself.

geographic_scope: Ranga/Red Soil mass grave site outside El Geneina
excerpt: OHCHR states that the bodies of seven women were among those buried in the mass grave.
transform_note: Incident-specific subgroup datapoint only.
confidence_note: High confidence for the listed grave-site victims.

geographic_scope: Ranga/Red Soil mass grave site outside El Geneina
excerpt: OHCHR states that the bodies of seven children were among those buried in the mass grave.
transform_note: Incident-specific subgroup datapoint only.
confidence_note: High confidence for the listed grave-site victims.

excerpt: HRW reports local estimates that the Sudanese Alliance fielded 1,000-2,000 men and Massalit self-defense groups another 500-1,000 in El Geneina.
transform_note: Low/best/high aggregate the two local anti-RSF components into a single anti-RSF force-presence range.
confidence_note: Reasonable for anti-RSF local fighters; not a total all-sides combatant count.

excerpt: Six Masalit fighters told Reuters that about 2,000 armed Masalit men deployed to protect civilians sheltering in the area.
transform_note: Used as a local-sector combatant-presence anchor, not as a whole-city all-sides total.
confidence_note: Moderate; witness-based but specific.

geographic_scope: Ourang camp households originating from El Geneina
excerpt: The survey found that 141 of 5,782 listed El Geneina-origin household members in Ourang were reported to have died from violent causes, or about 2.4%.
transform_note: Used as a severity/plausibility check rather than as the citywide fatality numerator.
confidence_note: Peer-reviewed and useful, but not a full-population city estimate.

excerpt: HRW relays a UNHCR estimate that about 800 people were killed during the early-November attacks in Ardamata.
transform_note: Used as the conservative lower bound for episode-wide civilian deaths.
confidence_note: Good low bound, but likely incomplete.

excerpt: HRW says local rights monitors interviewing survivors in Chad estimated the death toll of mainly civilians at between 1,300 and 2,000, including dozens killed on the road to Chad.
transform_note: Best value is the midpoint of the externally reported rights-monitor range.
confidence_note: Useful upper-middle civilian range, but not a registry.

excerpt: HRW reports that at least 8,000 people fled into Chad after the early-November attacks.
transform_note: Used as an immediate-outflow anchor for the Ardamata civilian denominator.
confidence_note: Strong minimum outflow number.

excerpt: Reuters says the assault hit many of the tens of thousands of people living in Ardamata's camp and surrounding neighborhoods, most of them Masalit.
transform_note: Operationalized the phrase 'tens of thousands' as a broad 20k-80k civilian presence range.
confidence_note: Necessary but weak denominator input.

geographic_scope: Ardamata and mountain route to Chad
excerpt: Reuters reports that a contingent of about 2,000 soldiers and fighters fled with civilians through the mountains toward Chad after the base fell.
transform_note: Treated as a defender-side force-presence anchor only; total all-sides combatant exposure must be higher.
confidence_note: Useful lower anchor for organized armed presence.

excerpt: A surviving soldier told Reuters that at least six of his colleagues were killed in the attack on the base.
transform_note: Minimum direct military-death datapoint, clearly incomplete.
confidence_note: Useful as a floor, not a full military toll.

excerpt: ACJPS reports that approximately 870 people, including soldiers, lost their lives in the Ardamata attacks, and also notes about 500 prisoners of war.
transform_note: Classified as mixed mortality because the source blends soldiers and civilians and does not resolve status shares cleanly.
confidence_note: Useful for bracketing but not directly ingestible into the core DR without decomposition.

geographic_scope: Arab neighborhoods in/near El Geneina linked to Ardamata fighting
excerpt: ACJPS reports that SAF shelling on November 3 killed 15 residents in predominantly Arab neighborhoods and injured 18 others, including children.
transform_note: Included to make clear that not every civilian death in the broader Ardamata window is attributable to RSF/allied forces.
confidence_note: Incident-specific and secondary to the main massacre series.

excerpt: HRW reports OHCHR's finding that 66 Massalit men were summarily executed in three incidents on November 5.
transform_note: Incident count useful for confirming mass-execution dynamics but not a full episode total.
confidence_note: Strong attributable incident count.

geographic_scope: Darfur and eastern Chad refugee spillover
excerpt: CRED's summary states that about 35,000 of the 120,000 conflict-attributable deaths in its Sep 2003-Jan 2005 window were violent deaths.
transform_note: Useful for the Darfur core window, but not status-separated into civilian versus combatant deaths.
confidence_note: Good violent-death anchor; inadequate for DR by itself.

geographic_scope: Darfur plus refugees in eastern Chad
excerpt: CRED notes the affected population rose from under 500,000 in September 2003 to about 2.4 million by January 2005, including Darfur and eastern Chad refugees.
transform_note: Best value uses a simple midpoint for context only; not a finalized person-month estimate.
confidence_note: Strong context for why a Darfur denominator exists, but not enough for a final DR without combatant exposure.

excerpt: The Khartoum multiple-systems estimation study reports 61,202 all-cause deaths in the first 14 months of the war, with a very wide confidence interval.
transform_note: Classified as mixed mortality context because all-cause mortality cannot enter the core direct-death DR.
confidence_note: Important but not directly DR-ingestible.

excerpt: The same study estimates 26,024 intentional-injury deaths in Khartoum State, but does not separate civilian from combatant victims.
transform_note: Strong direct-violence context, but not status separated enough for a DR numerator.
confidence_note: Best available Khartoum direct-violence figure, still insufficient for publication of DR.

excerpt: Reuters, citing OHCHR, reports at least 782 civilians killed and more than 1,143 injured in El Fasher since May 2024.
transform_note: Good siege-specific civilian numerator context, but still ongoing and incomplete.
confidence_note: Useful for the proposed episode; not enough to publish a DR during an evolving siege.
